reasoning: XLF down -0.54% (crosses risk-off threshold) + XLE down -0.82% indicates financial sector stress and energy weakness. However, XLK remains flat (-0.06%) and SPY itself is essentially flat (-0.08%), limiting conviction. This is modest downside bias from sector-specific weakness in financials rather than broad-based SPY selling. The rotation is risk-off but incomplete — tech is not yet participating in the selloff.
key_observations: XLF -0.54%: Financial sector weakness crossing the -0.5% threshold — classic risk-off signal indicating stress/defensiveness
XLE -0.82%: Energy sector weakness, coordinated with financials to reinforce risk-off lean
XLK -0.06%: Tech holding flat while financials and energy sell — relative outperformance but no positive leadership
Overall rotation: Risk-off momentum from prior close, but low magnitude and incomplete (tech not following). SPY resilience (flat) suggests rotation may not be SPY-driven.
